title: Human-Computer Interaction
description: Study of human-computer interaction principles, user interface design, usability evaluation, and user experience research. Covers design thinking, user-centered design process, prototyping (paper and digital), heuristic evaluation, usability testing, accessibility (WCAG guidelines), and interaction design patterns. Students complete a semester-long UX project including user research, iterative prototyping, and usability testing with real users. Prerequisite: CS102 provides programming skills needed for implementing interactive prototypes.
outcomes: Conduct user research using interviews and surveys
Perform heuristic evaluation of user interfaces
Conduct usability tests and analyze results
Design accessible interfaces following WCAG guidelines
